Class InMemoryBackend

java.lang.Object
chat.server.InMemoryBackend
All Implemented Interfaces:
Backend
public class InMemoryBackend extends Object implements Backend
Simple in-memory Backend implementation backed by concurrent maps.

This backend stores the set of reserved nicknames and the latest PrintWriter associated with each connected client. It is intended for testing and local demos.

      public InMemoryBackend()

  • Method Details

    • reserveNick

      public boolean reserveNick(String nick)
      Attempts to reserve a nickname for a connecting client.
      Specified by:
      reserveNick in interface Backend
      Parameters:
      nick - the requested nickname
      Returns:
      true if successful, false if the nickname is already taken

    • releaseNick

      public void releaseNick(String nick)
      Releases a previously reserved nickname. Implementations should be idempotent.
      Specified by:
      releaseNick in interface Backend
      Parameters:
      nick - the nickname to release

    • attachWriter

      public void attachWriter(String nick, PrintWriter out)
      Associates the given writer with the specified nickname so future messages can be delivered.
      Parameters:
      nick - the nickname for which to attach the writer
      out - a live PrintWriter connected to the client's socket

    • broadcast

      public void broadcast(String fromNick, String text)
      Broadcasts a public message to all connected clients.
      Specified by:
      broadcast in interface Backend
      Parameters:
      fromNick - sender nickname
      text - message text

    • sendPrivate

      public boolean sendPrivate(String fromNick, String toNick, String text)
      Sends a private message to a specific recipient.
      Specified by:
      sendPrivate in interface Backend
      Parameters:
      fromNick - sender nickname
      toNick - recipient nickname
      text - message text
      Returns:
      true if the receiver exists and the message was queued for delivery; false otherwise

    • usersCsv

      public String usersCsv()
      Returns the list of reserved nicknames as a comma-separated string.
      Specified by:
      usersCsv in interface Backend
      Returns:
      e.g., "alice,bob"; empty string if there are no users

    • broadcastUsersList

      public void broadcastUsersList()
      Broadcasts the current users list to all connected clients.
      Specified by:
      broadcastUsersList in interface Backend
